metrics.narrative: Andrew Puente articulated qualitative pain on the 13 Apr discovery call: Dover's highly decentralized procurement (50–60 ERP systems, frequent M&A, autonomous OpCos) makes enterprise-wide tools 'highly unlikely' due to integration cost and change-management resistance. Prior S2C tool failed on 'lack of integration... not enough support in terms of implementation and rollout... getting to any kind of tool that is enterprise wide is always a challenge.' Andrew is seeking 'a solution that doesn't require a significant amount of investment or effort and provides benefit back to the operating companies'—modular, opt-in, self-service (e.g. supplier selection, market intelligence, sourcing tools OpCos can adopt when needed). Brittany added internal segmentation example: Dover Food Retail alone runs AS400, Visual, SyteLine across 3 sites. Pain is confirmed and well-articulated. GAP: no quantified savings, cost impact, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement stated. Andrew has not quantified the cost of the failed S2C tool, the manual effort in the current data-lake normalization process, or the tail-spend leakage Dover is experiencing. Zycus positioned ANA as controlling 'up to 80% of unmanaged tail spending' but Andrew did not validate Dover's tail-spend volume or leakage rate. Quantifying the business case (savings from tail-spend automation, RFx efficiency, data-lake replacement) is the next step to build EB-grade ROI justification.

champion.narrative: No confirmed champion. Andrew Puente (Sr Dir Global Sourcing, Decision Maker contact role) is engaged and driving the evaluation ('I accepted the meeting just to try and catch up on what Zycus has been doing and potentially where there may be a solution that fits Dover'), but on 13 Apr he was in information-collection mode, not advocacy. He acknowledged Zycus positioning ('it makes sense') and requested collateral to review internally ('I'll probably have to go through this a little bit more and get back to you'), but has not introduced Zycus to other stakeholders (EB, IT, OpCo leads), shared internal urgency/timeline, or pushed back on competitors (none surfaced yet). Brittany Quiroz (Sourcing Director, Dover Food Retail OpCo) was present as 'support' but passive ('I don't, but I'm just here for support. So Andrew and then whatever I need to do'—she deferred to Andrew throughout). DEVELOPING: Andrew's pain is real and he is actively evaluating; collateral delivered ~9 Jun, fit-check call scheduling in progress (Next Step 17 Jun). To convert Andrew to champion: (1) on fit-check call, demonstrate Teams interface + AppXtend ERP-connector ease (prove low-friction claim he needs to sell internally), (2) ask Andrew to bring Greg Weddle (his boss, VP Global Sourcing) or an OpCo peer to the demo so multi-threading begins, (3) surface his internal timeline and whether he is evaluating other vendors (competition unknown). Champion-building is the top priority after the fit-check call.

decision_criteria.narrative: Andrew stated evaluation criteria on 13 Apr: (1) modular/opt-in (not mandatory enterprise-wide rollout—'something that's a little bit uniform or standard... but not necessarily something where we're telling the operating companies they have to use it'), (2) low integration burden (must work with 50–60 ERPs without 'millions of dollars' investment), (3) low change management (prior tool failed on 'not enough support in terms of implementation and rollout'), (4) immediate OpCo value (supplier selection, market intelligence, sourcing tools 'there for their use if they need it'). Brittany's question 'is it like all or nothing or can you like pick the areas that you need?' confirms modularity is a decision gate. Zycus positioned Microsoft Teams interface (90%+ adoption claim, no new system to learn), AppXtend (1,000+ ERP connectors), and modular pricing as meeting criteria 1–3. Andrew acknowledged 'it makes sense' but has not validated via demo or pilot. INFERENCE: additional unstated criteria likely include (a) IT/InfoSec approval of architecture and security posture (Mike Skibo VP IT, Rahul Naroola CISO not yet engaged), (b) proof of low-friction adoption (Teams interface demo, reference calls), (c) cost vs ROI (Andrew: 'doesn't require a significant amount of investment'—pricing sensitivity). Fit-check call is the venue to surface and confirm the full scorecard.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage enterprise S2P deal with solid buyer pain (decentralized 50–60 ERPs, failed prior S2C tool, OpCo autonomy) but major gaps block progression: EB not mapped, IT/InfoSec not engaged, champion unconfirmed, competition unknown, quantified ROI missing. Fit-check call is the gate to multi-thread, validate fit, surface competition, and tee up demo. Win strategy: (1) convert Andrew to champion by proving Teams ease + AppXtend integration (his stated pain), (2) multi-thread to EB (CFO/VP Procurement), IT (Skibo), and OpCo peer on demo, (3) run quantified ROI workshop (tail-spend savings, RFx efficiency) to justify $150k to EB, (4) engage IT/InfoSec early to de-risk integration gate. Current risk: single-threaded to Andrew (business buyer, not budget holder), positioning blind (no competition intel), and no exec sponsorship.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor named or logged. At this Qualified stage (2.5 months in, 175 days to close) the absence is not alarming — buyer is in discovery/education, and Andrew said he is 'just collecting information,' not running a formal RFP. Risk: we do not know if Dover is benchmarking Zycus against SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, a niche integration/tail-spend player, or considering do-nothing (status quo: data lake + decentralized OpCo procurement). Prior S2C tool failure ('lack of integration... not enough support') means scar tissue and caution; any competitor claiming easier integration or faster time-to-value is a threat. Wedge (if competing): AppXtend (1,000+ ERP connectors, organic codebase, lower TCO vs stitched platforms), Microsoft Teams interface (90%+ adoption, minimal change mgmt vs standalone portals), Merlin/ANA agentic AI (tail-spend automation, end-to-end orchestration vs copilots). Fit-check call must surface competitive landscape ('Who else are you looking at?') — until then, we are positioning blind. Current threat: UNKNOWN.
